name: Ronin Bridge
defensive_control: Runtime signer threshold health checks, collateral vs mint monitoring
summary: Attacker gained control of 5/9 validator keys and withdrew funds without corresponding deposits.

name: Wormhole
root_cause_category: Signature verification bypass / missing invariant check
defensive_control: Mint-lock invariant check, guardian-set cooldown monitoring
summary: Exploit allowed minting of wrapped ETH without providing collateral on Solana.

name: Nomad
defensive_control: Duplicate message detection, strict nonce handling
summary: A misconfigured acceptableRoot check allowed anyone to forge messages and replay them.

name: Harmony Horizon
defensive_control: Validator set risk monitoring, forced rotation on weak thresholds
summary: Attacker compromised two of four multisig keys and drained the bridge.

name: BNB Token Hub
defensive_control: Message uniqueness, proof verification at runtime
summary: Attacker crafted a fake proof to mint 2M BNB on BSC.

name: Poly Network
summary: Attacker used leaked keeper keys to change the bridge keeper set and drain assets.

name: Multichain
defensive_control: MPC node health monitoring, TVL divergence alerting
summary: Unusual large withdrawals without corresponding deposits, suspected insider.

name: THORChain recent incident
defensive_control: Emergency mode enforcement, invariant checks during pause
summary: During emergency pause, a bug allowed a user to extract funds beyond burned collateral.

name: Orbit Chain
root_cause_category: Compromised validator keys / weak access control
defensive_control: Signer threshold health monitoring, volume anomaly detection
summary: Attacker gained control of several validator keys and drained assets. Volume spike and weak signer configuration were detectable early.

name: Socket/Bungee
root_cause_category: Insufficient input validation / user‑supplied route manipulation
defensive_control: Route caps, cooldown on route additions, payload validation
summary: A malicious route was injected, allowing siphoning of user funds. Route caps and config cooldown would have blocked the abnormal path.

name: Heco Bridge (HTX)
defensive_control: Runtime mint‑lock checks, signer health monitoring, TVL oracle
summary: Large unauthorized minting of wrapped assets on Heco without corresponding collateral on Ethereum.

name: Mixin Network
root_cause_category: Cloud provider compromise / lack of finality verification
summary: Attackers compromised the cloud database and submitted fake deposits. Finality checks would have delayed the transfers.

name: LI.FI protocol
defensive_control: Duplicate message detection, rate limiting, approval revoke monitoring
summary: Unlimited approvals allowed an attacker to repeatedly call the bridge and drain user funds. Duplicate detection would have blocked the replay.

name: Celer cBridge DNS hijack
defensive_control: Trusted root monitoring, config change cooldown, DNS/SSL pinning alerts
summary: DNS was hijacked to display a malicious frontend. Backend invariant checks would have detected sudden root changes and escalated.

name: Kelp DAO rsETH Bridge
root_cause_category: Forged cross-chain message / access control bypass
defensive_control: Message validation, governance health monitoring, TVL divergence alerting
summary: Attackers exploited a flaw in cross-chain messaging to forge a message that minted 116,500 rsETH without corresponding collateral, impacting multiple protocols.

name: Shibarium Bridge
defensive_control: Validator set monitoring, flash loan anomaly detection, rate limiting
summary: Attacker used a $4.6M flash loan to gain temporary validator control, draining the bridge contract.

name: Nervos Force Bridge
summary: Attacker exploited insufficient validation to mint wrapped assets on Nervos without proving a valid deposit on the source chain.

name: Verus Ethereum Bridge
root_cause_category: Verification bypass / release without proof of backing
defensive_control: Burn-release invariant enforcement, source chain finality checks
summary: The bridge released funds on Ethereum without properly verifying they were locked or burned on the Verus side, still ongoing as of May 18, 2026.

name: NoOnes Solana Bridge
summary: The Solana bridge infrastructure of P2P platform NoOnes was compromised, leading to the theft of $8M in assets. Details remained undisclosed.
